column1: A Tornado Warning has been issued for our area. Stay tuned for further updates on your ABC Action News Station.
Prayers for all of FL being impacted by this Storm!
We're used to this, and it's only a tropical storm ... 🙂 There's another one behind it, Denis Phillips will be busy this season for sure!! Mother Nature has awoken !

oh really. Didn't hear that. Guess I've been too busy wondering about this storm and busy with other things. Definitely need to keep watching. Sounds like it will be busy the next 3 months with storms. Praying nothing happens with these storms.
Oh I know, Im a native Floridian. Born and raised here. Never hurts to pray.
Thank you🙏🏻🙏🏻🙏🏻

Hi everyone — can someone give us some advice? We are staying at a condo right on the beach in siesta key and need to check out by 10am. Our flight isn’t until 9pm. Where is the safest place to go between now and then???
maybe a mall in Sarasota area, or head to airport (just trying to think of large spaces with food options etc)
I would get another hotel. No way is a flight headed out tonight if your on the west coast.
Pretty sure by tonite its gonna be gone
from your area yes...

they'll re-route as much as they can to get flights out. It's not hitting bend til tomorrow, so they can go around some bands. Stopping all flights would be a nightmare for the airport and days of ppl stranded.
Agreed…. If they will let you book another night. Or stay at the airport. Very doubtful there will be flights tonight or tomorrow morning.

I never said they wouldn't cancel flight?...so not sure what you're getting at. 🤷‍♀️ they're going to take off as long as they can. They aren't going to shut it done for 24 hrs because of rain. These outer bands are basic storms. They reroute as much as they can.

I would go closer to tampa. If the gusts get strong enough, they'll close down the skyway bridge. It will just be more traffic, time consuming for you all getting to the airport. Go closer to Brandon mall, go to a late lunch, movie, etc. Otherwise you'd be fine. Just avoid the traffic stresses. 🙂
**oops...just assumed it was tampa, since its my go to airport...🤣 so if so, I'd do that. If sarasota or south of skyway, I'd probably do same thing. Wander around mall, can relax in massage chairs for a long day, movies, etc. Enjoy your last day!
If your flight goes out the airport is safe. Being on the road is prob the most dangerous part.

sorry for the extra excitement with your vacation. Keep in contact with your airline about your flight.
Consider keeping to stores and restaurants near the airport. Be prepared to book a hotel for the night.
Checking in from our roadtrip in Canada as we watch out for our home in The Villages. Thanks for the continued updates! 🙏🙏🙏
Nothing Clearwater beach side. Watched the Dolphins play in Tropical Storm Debby
Have a feeling it will happen multiple times today!
Stay safe people!
I'm stuck waiting for crew at the airport

I hope people stay safe and are prepared just in case, so many down play it like its just a tropical rain storm .... They said the same when IAN turned at the last minute and hit like a vengeance. So far Pinellas looks like they will be spared but it can change in an instant.
We are between Sarasota and St. Petersburg.
it’s gonna get 🌧️
I’m in Arcadia snd my phone about jumped out of my hand when alert came in….so far , so good but Desoto a big county
I better get the dog out for a walk really quick

it's going up to the Big Bend area. NHC shows us getting lots of rain, and gusts up to 40 mph.

I checked NOAA and we are not in that path

yes but still upon first reading it the term our area is very broad

Being out on the road, crossing bridges, etc is not safe. Stay in your hotel lobby or find another hotel close by. If you are near a mall that's another option.
Thanks again
Stay safe.
We are easy for the surge.
where did you get these please?
its called Dam Easy. We had it installed by JMI windows who is a contractor for them. Send me a message through messenger if you want the contact info.
They are not removable?
they are removable. This was before.
just curious , how does each section seal? Rubber gaskets on 3 edges, and what keeps the water from collapsing multiple sections? Great decision

yes, there is a pull which expands the sides to each post. Then a pump to fill the gaskets with air to create the seal. They are designed to withstand 150 mph winds. Each post is screw’s into hole that are drilled in the concrete.
wish my sons mother in law would invest, on inner coastal in StPetes beach.Nail biter every surge forecast. Dont understand on a 800K home.
it took awhile for my mother in law to do this. After Idailia last year and 4-6 inches of water and us convincing her she agreed.
